overrides.override_summary: Post-term covenant not to compete may not be enforceable under California law.
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees may be able to compete immediately after franchise termination without restriction.

overrides.override_summary: Interest rate capped at 10% APR (or 5% + Federal Reserve rate, whichever is higher), overriding the 18% APR in the standard FA.
overrides.why_it_matters: Late payment penalties are substantially reduced for California franchisees.

overrides.override_summary: Cure periods in sections 7.2(d), 13.1, and 13.3 extended from 30 days to 60 days.
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees get double the cure time before termination can take effect.

overrides.override_summary: California Franchise Investment Law and California Franchise Relations Act govern, regardless of PA choice of law in standard FA.
overrides.why_it_matters: California's more protective franchise laws apply, potentially limiting franchisor's termination and non-renewal rights.

overrides.override_summary: General release upon transfer excludes rights under CA Franchise Investment Law and CA Franchise Relations Act.
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees cannot be forced to waive franchise law protections as condition of transfer.

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clause noted with reference to Civil Code Section 1671 enforceability standards.
overrides.why_it_matters: Lost royalty recovery formula may be challengeable under CA liquidated damages standards.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor may have obligation to purchase franchisee assets if franchisee does not continue real estate brokerage at location after termination/non-renewal.
overrides.why_it_matters: Provides California franchisees with potential asset buyback protection not available in other states.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may sue in Maryland for claims arising under the Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law. 3-year statute of limitations.
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees are not required to travel to PA for claims under their own state's franchise law.

overrides.override_summary: General release required for renewal/sale/transfer does not apply to liability under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law. Release/estoppel/waiver provisions do not affect MD law liability.
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees retain all rights under state franchise law regardless of releases signed.

overrides.override_summary: Maryland Securities Commissioner requires financial assurance: all initial fees deferred until franchisor completes pre-opening obligations. Based on franchisor's financial condition.
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees do not pay initial fees until franchisor performs — mitigating financial condition risk.

overrides.override_summary: Termination only for good cause, with 30-day cure. Franchisee cannot waive rights under Michigan Franchise Investment Law.
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan franchisees have stronger termination protections than standard FA provides.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisor must fairly compensate franchisee on non-renewal by repurchasing inventory, supplies, equipment, fixtures at fair market value (conditions: term <5 years and franchisee prohibited from continuing under another brand or received <6 months notice).
overrides.why_it_matters: Provides asset protection for Michigan franchisees whose franchise is not renewed, under certain conditions.

overrides.override_summary: Franchise documents may not require arbitration or litigation outside Michigan.
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan franchisees cannot be forced to arbitrate in Philadelphia County, PA.

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee cannot be required to assent to release, assignment, novation, waiver, or estoppel that deprives rights under Michigan Act.
overrides.why_it_matters: Anti-waiver protection for Michigan franchise law rights.

overrides.override_summary: Cancellation without reasonable cause is unlawful under Virginia Retail Franchising Act (Section 13.1-564).
overrides.why_it_matters: Virginia provides statutory protection against arbitrary termination beyond FA terms.

overrides.override_summary: Virginia SCC requires deferral of initial franchise fee and other initial payments until franchisor completes pre-opening obligations.
overrides.why_it_matters: Virginia franchisees' initial fees are protected until franchisor performs — similar to Maryland financial condition requirement.

overrides.override_summary: Washington Franchise Investment Protection Act (RCW 19.100) prevails in event of conflict with franchise agreement.
overrides.why_it_matters: WA law provides additional protections in termination and renewal beyond what PA law offers.

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation must be in Washington or mutually agreed location. Litigation may be brought in Washington for WA Act claims.
overrides.why_it_matters: Washington franchisees are not required to travel to PA for dispute resolution.

overrides.override_summary: Release/waiver excludes rights under WA Franchise Investment Protection Act. Provisions restricting statute of limitations, rights, or remedies (including jury trial) may be unenforceable.
overrides.why_it_matters: Broad anti-waiver protection for WA franchisees.

overrides.override_summary: Post-term noncompete void for employees earning <$100,000/year and independent contractors earning <$250,000/year (adjusted annually for inflation). No-poach clause void: franchisor cannot restrict franchisee from soliciting/hiring other franchisees' or franchisor's employees.
overrides.why_it_matters: Limits noncompete enforcement for most real estate agents and eliminates no-poach restrictions.

overrides.override_summary: Initial franchise fees deferred until franchisee has received all pre-opening training obligations and is open for business. ADRSA fees released proportionally per outlet.
overrides.why_it_matters: Most protective fee deferral — franchisee pays nothing until fully trained and open.

overrides.override_summary: WI Fair Dealership Law prohibits termination, cancellation, nonrenewal, or substantial change of competitive circumstances without good cause. 90-day written notice required. 60-day cure period.
overrides.why_it_matters: Wisconsin provides the longest notice period (90 days vs. 30 in standard FA) and longest cure period (60 days) of any state addendum.

overrides.override_summary: WI Fair Dealership Law (Chapter 135) supersedes conflicting FA provisions.
overrides.why_it_matters: WI statutory protections override PA choice of law for Wisconsin franchisees.

template_errors.error: Addendum references 'Realty ONE Group Affiliates, Inc.' instead of 'Iron Valley Real Estate, LLC'
